text: Hardens a-0020 on actual billed cost. (1) The e-0016 list-price over-prediction is fully explained: harness-billed total_cost_usd is a near-constant 0.335x of the four-term list-price reconstruction (median 0.333, sd 0.009), INVARIANT across all toolsets (0.333-0.340) and rungs (0.333-0.338) — a global ~1/3 discount, not a structured error, so every ratio/shape claim in a-0015/a-0020 is price-discount-invariant. (2) The mechanism survives 3 cell-means -> 42 trials: corr(list net_mech, billed gap)=0.923, R^2=0.84; fitted per-OUT-saved-token coef 2.46e-5 = 0.33x list, tracking the 0.335 discount, confirming the externalization-saving term is the cost driver (tax term weak at trial resolution). (3) The single-peaked Goldilocks shape is statistically real: tier-2 net_mech 95% CI [3.48,8.97] excludes 0 while tier-1/tier-3 include 0; tier-2 is the peak in 100% of 10k bootstraps. Caveat: 3 puzzles, single Opus-4.7 model.
